Media
=====

Media
----------------

Media
expand\_more

**February 3, 2026:** Featured interview in Genomic Psychiatry’s “Innovators & Ideas” interview series: Genomic Psychiatry published an in-depth interview with Professor Kessler about his career. The interview is available here: <https://doi.org/10.61373/gp026k.0021>

**September 9, 2025:** Prof. Kessler served on the panel, "Suicide Prevention for Service Members and Veterans," at the 2nd Annual Center for Suicide Research and Prevention Conference. Video available below.

**May 6, 2025:** Prof. Kessler presented at the UNC Suicide Prevention Institute. Video available below.

**December 13, 2024:** Prof. Kessler was interviewed by the Center for Suicide Research and Prevention on the webinar "Machine Learning-Based Prediction Models for Suicide Prevention: *An Expert Discussion of the Greatest Opportunities and Challenges.*" To listen to the interview, watch below.

**July 24, 2024:** Interview with Robert D. Gibbons, PhD, and Ronald C. Kessler, PhD, authors of "Social Vulnerability and Prevalence and Treatment for Mental Health and Substance Use Disorders". Hosted by John Torous, MD, MBI. You can listen to the episode [here](https://edhub.ama-assn.org/jn-learning/audio-player/18898356).

**February 6, 2024:** Prof. Kessler appeared on the NYU Langone *Insights on Psychiatry*Podcast, Episode "Precision Psychiatry (with Ronald Kessler, PhD)." You can listen to the episode and view the transcript [here](https://www.buzzsprout.com/2122717/14447934-precision-psychiatry-with-ronald-kessler-phd). The episode is also available on [Apple Podcasts](https://podcasts.apple.com/us/podcast/precision-psychiatry-with-ronald-kessler-phd/id1672458622?i=1000644332863), [Spotify](https://open.spotify.com/episode/6LPUB6OL0goCcyyihwO1aN?si=h5A4UYexRR-GeGwLXJqCSw), and other major podcast platforms.

**January 29, 2019:** Prof. Kessler was interviewed on Radio New Zealand’s Nine to Noon program to discuss the social determinants of mental health. To listen to the interview, click [here](https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__www.radionz.co.nz_national_programmes_ninetonoon_audio_2018680178_the-2Dsocial-2Ddeterminants-2Dof-2Dmental-2Dhealth&d=DwMFAg&c=WO-RGvefibhHBZq3fL85hQ&r=nn0rHH3BLH1VjxuVtkwjCBe-CH7TYVCMgTGPyJclNHQ&m=p7x5stP1mOghJMVYAgNN-KBou6DgnAydcm3RIHd12fA&s=wPshNs5fzH_GA1hkRB-PuhXrIjVIyJdM9Sd9SCqNX24&e=).

**December 4, 2019:** Prof. Kessler testified before the Senate Armed Services Subcommittee on Personnel at a hearing on military suicide prevention. A recording of the testimony can be viewed [here](https://www.c-span.org/video/?467033-1/soldier-veteran-suicide-prevention&live=). You can also read the transcript of the testimony here and view questions for the record (QFR) from the testimony here.

!Prof. Kessler testifying

**October 30, 2019:** Prof. Kessler served on the panel, "Building the Mental Health System we Need," at the Milken Insitute Future of Health Summit. Video available [here.](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7CEOEcDHhZc&feature=youtu.be)

**October 18, 2019:** Prof. Kessler presented at MIT Fail! event. Video available below.

**October 4, 2019:** Prof. Kessler presented at the 12th Congress of the Italian Society of Biological Psychiatry. Video available [here](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=A0tleDTu24w).

**April 22, 2019:** Prof. Kessler was elected to the 239th class of the American Academy of Arts and Sciences. To read more, click [here](https://www.hcp.med.harvard.edu/node/6652). Additional information [here](https://hms.harvard.edu/news/four-hms-elected-american-academy-arts-sciences).

**March 15, 2019:** Coverage for recently published paper Zuromski et al. paper “[Assessment of a Risk Index for Suicide Attempts Among US Army Soldiers With Suicide Ideation Analysis of Data From the Army Study to Assess Risk and Resilience in Servicemembers (Army STARRS).](https://jamanetwork.com/journals/jamanetworkopen/fullarticle/2728008)” Healio Psychiatric Annals “[Self-report battery may improve risk assessment in soldiers with suicidal ideation](https://www.healio.com/psychiatry/suicide/news/online/%7B6f787111-aa34-44c6-a423-471ac74ef4a3%7D/self-report-battery-may-improve-risk-assessment-in-soldiers-with-suicidal-ideation).” Note that free registration may be required to access the article.

**October 10, 2017:** The National Academy of Sciences featured a post by Prof. Kessler discussing World Mental Health Day. To read more, click [here](https://www.facebook.com/theNASciences/posts/1136126796528382).

**November 10, 2002:** 60 Minutes featured Prof. Kessler in their episode "Depressed and on Welfare"
